title: First When Maggy Was My Care.
content:
Tune - "Whistle o'er the lave o't."


I.

        First when Maggy was my care,
        Heaven, I thought, was in her air;
        Now we're married, spier nae mair,
            Whistle o'er the lave o't.
        Meg was meek, and Meg was mild,
        Bonnie Meg was nature's child;
        Wiser men than me's beguil'd,
            Whistle o'er the lave o't.

II.

        How we live, my Meg and me,
        How we love, and how we 'gree,
        I care na by how few may see;
            Whistle o'er the lave o't.
        Wha I wish were maggot's meat,
        Dish'd up in her winding sheet,
        I could write, but Meg maun see't,
            Whistle o'er the lave o't.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Burns delivers a powerful performance in "First When Maggy Was My Care."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
